title: Phase 1D: expand ferrotorch-core::special with ferray-polynomial orthogonal polynomial families
description:
Wire ferray-polynomial into ferrotorch-core::special to close the long-tail polynomial gap flagged in docs/audit/01-ferrotorch-core.md.

ferray-polynomial provides Chebyshev (T/U/V/W), Hermite (physicists + probabilists), Laguerre, Legendre, plus power, fitting, roots.

Functions to add (mirror torch.special naming):
- chebyshev_polynomial_t/u/v/w (ferray: chebyshev module)
- hermite_polynomial_h (ferray: hermite)
- hermite_polynomial_he (ferray: hermite_e)
- laguerre_polynomial_l (ferray: laguerre)
- legendre_polynomial_p (ferray: legendre)
- shifted_chebyshev_polynomial_t/u/v/w (ferray polynomial mapping helper covers x in [0,1] case)

Scope:
- Add ferray-polynomial = workspace dep in ferrotorch-core/Cargo.toml.
- Add a polynomials submodule under ferrotorch-core/src/special/ (split special.rs if it grows past ~400 LOC).
- Each fn takes Tensor<T> + integer order n; returns Tensor<T> evaluated pointwise.
- Re-export from lib.rs.
- Tests: small-order closed-form checks (T_2(x)=2x^2-1, P_2(x)=(3x^2-1)/2, etc.).

GPU discipline (apply /rust-gpu-discipline pre-flight):
- torch.special polynomial functions DO have GPU paths via dispatched kernels. Adding a CPU-only path here is acceptable for Phase 1D ONLY if the function is gated to Device::Cpu and returns FerrotorchError::DeviceUnavailable for GPU tensors with a clear message naming what is missing.
- Forbidden: silently calling t.to(Device::Cpu) before delegating to ferray. That bounces GPU tensors through host without the user noticing — exactly the failure mode /rust-gpu-discipline is meant to catch.
- Open a follow-up issue tagged 'gpu' for adding cubecl kernels that evaluate the recurrences on device. The recurrences are all of form P_n+1 = a_n*x*P_n - b_n*P_n-1, easy to vectorize with shared-memory accumulators. Track but do NOT implement in Phase 1D.
- Document in the module docstring: 'Currently CPU-only. GPU tensors return DeviceUnavailable. See issue #XXX for cubecl polynomial kernels.'

Closes the 'orthogonal polynomial families' tail of the torch.special gap.
